title: Locking Item name so it cannot be edited
excerpt:
Description
Update the “Permission” settings to allow the control of what users are able to edit an Item name 
What are you trying to achieve
I would like to see the ability to lock an item name so users cannot edit it. …

posts.author: RowanThompson (Rowan Thompson)
posts.cookedHtml:
Description

Update the “Permission” settings to allow the control of what users are able to edit an Item name

What are you trying to achieve

I would like to see the ability to lock an item name so users cannot edit it. At present, due to the ability to edit item names, it is causing automations that reference the item name to fail as the item name has been edited.

posts.author: YOGESHP (Yogesh Pokale)
posts.cookedHtml:
RowanThompson:

I would like to see the ability to lock an item name so users cannot edit it. At present, due to the ability to edit item names, it is causing automations that reference the item name to fail as the item name has been edited.

Hi @RowanThompson, Welcome to the community!

This is a great request.

Locking the Item Name column is currently not supported, as it is a core system column and cannot have column-level permissions applied. Because of this, editing the Item Name causes automations that reference it to fail.

In the meantime, here are some workarounds you can try:

  • Create a separate “Reference ID” text column, restrict edit access to it (on supported plans), and update automations to reference that field instead of the Item Name.

  • Use a stable identifier (e.g., Item ID or a unique key column) in automations rather than the Item Name.

  • Limit board editing permissions to reduce unintended name changes.

posts.author: lAMhUAN21 (hUAN)
posts.cookedHtml: I’ve run into the same issue before. Locking the item name would definitely help when automations depend on it, because even a small change can break the workflow. For now the only workaround I’ve seen is limiting who can edit items or using a separate column (like an ID) for automations instead of the item name. Hopefully Monday adds this kind of permission control in the future.

posts.author: RowanThompson (Rowan Thompson)
posts.cookedHtml: Thank you for acknowledging my request. I was trying to avoid the need to create additional columns due to the complexity of the workflow we use. But, with this explination and the low level liklihood of a update being applied any time in the near future and the frequency in this issue occuring we have no other choice other than to create new columns as the reference ID and point our automations at that

posts.author: ProjectMastermind (IngenuityKat)
posts.cookedHtml:
Yes — we need this so badly.

Project names get changed all the time, and it creates a surprising amount of upstream and downstream chaos.

It would be incredibly helpful to have the option to:

• Lock the item name entirely
• Or restrict editing to board owners/admins only

Right now the item name sits in this weird space where it’s both a label and a structural reference, and that’s where the friction comes from.

Being able to control who can edit it would solve a lot of headaches.
